initial_program:
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  The input facts are already given by the puzzle engine.
%  They are accessed through the predicate  input/3 .
%  The cell (r,c) contains the colour value  input(r,c,colour).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Dimensions of the input
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)    :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)} .
n_cols(M)    :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)} .

% Because the puzzle only uses one row, the maxdimension of the input
% equals the number of columns.
max_dim(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)} .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Number of nonzero cells
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
K(Kc)        :- Kc = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V), V != 0} .

% Output size = K  max_dim  (a square)
output_max(O) :- O = Kc * max_dim .
output_last(O) :- O = O - 1 .                % last index of the output

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper predicates for a nonzero cell at (0,c) with value V
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Z(c) = number of zeros in the same row that are left of column c
zero_before(c,Z) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Z = #count{C0 : C0 < c, input(0,C0,0)} .

%  extra = 1 if the column index c is > 0, otherwise 0
extra(c,Extra) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Extra = 1, c != 0 .
extra(c,Extra) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Extra = 0, c = 0 .

%  base = c  Z
base_val(c,Base) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Base = c - Zc .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Two ways of placing a copy of the input
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  1)  K > 1   the copy is placed in two antidiagonal blocks:
%       topright block  (row block = 0 , col block = (K1)max_dim)
%       bottomleft block(row block = (K1)max_dim , col block = 0)

%  2)  K = 1   the copy is placed on the antidiagonal of the whole
%       output (a diagonal line of length max_dim  start).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Block generation (used when K > 1)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
Kminus1 = Kc - 1 .
block_col_offset = (Kminus1) * max_dim .           % col block offset
block_row_offset = (Kminus1) * max_dim .           % row block offset

%  For each nonzero cell we create two positions:
%  topright  :  (row = base + extra , col = (K1)max_dim + (max_dim1base))
%  bottomleft:  (row = (K1)max_dim + (max_dim1baseextra) , col = base + extra)

nonzero_top(R,C,V) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    row_top = base + Extra,
    col_top = max_dim - 1 - base,
    block_col = (Kminus1) * max_dim,
    R = row_top,
    C = block_col + col_top,
    V = V .
nonzero_bottom(R,C,V) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    row_bot = max_dim - 1 - base - Extra,
    col_bot = base + Extra,
    block_row = (Kminus1) * max_dim,
    R = block_row + row_bot,
    C = col_bot,
    V = V .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Diagonal generation (used when K = 1)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  startRow = base + extra + 1
diag_start(c,Start) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    Start = base + Extra + 1,
    maxR = max_dim - 1 .

%  i is the offset along the diagonal (0  maxRstart)
i_offset(I) :-
    I = 0 .
i_offset(I) :-
    I0 = I1,
    I = I1 + 1,
    I <= max_dim - 1 - Start .
diag_nonzero(R,C,V) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    diag_start(c,Start),
    i_offset(I),
    R = Start + I,
    C = max_dim - 1 - I,
    V = V .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  has_nonzero  true if a nonzero colour must be placed here
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :-
    nonzero_top(R,_,_).
nonzero_bottom(R,_,_) :- true .   % every nonzero position will make has_nonzero true

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    diag_nonzero(R,C,Col), use_diag .
output(R,C,Col) :-
    nonzero_top(R,C,Col) ;   nonzero_bottom(R,C,Col) , not use_diag .
output(R,C,0) :-
    not has_nonzero(R,C) .      % all remaining cells stay 0

output(R,C) :-
    has_nonzero(R,C) .
output(R,C) :-
    not has_nonzero(R,C) .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cardinality  exactly one colour for each output cell
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output(R,C) .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary facts for the use_diag guard
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
use_diag :- Kc = 1 .
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour values (automatically supplied by the solver)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
colour(0..9) .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  The input facts are already given by the puzzle engine.
%  They are accessed through the predicate  input/3 .
%  The cell (r,c) contains the colour value  input(r,c,colour).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Dimensions of the input
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)    :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)} .
n_cols(M)    :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)} .

% Because the puzzle only uses one row, the maxdimension of the input
% equals the number of columns.
max_dim(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)} .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Number of nonzero cells
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
K(Kc)        :- Kc = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V), V != 0} .

% Output size = K  max_dim  (a square)
output_max(O) :- O = Kc * max_dim .
output_last(O) :- O = O - 1 .                % last index of the output

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper predicates for a nonzero cell at (0,c) with value V
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Z(c) = number of zeros in the same row that are left of column c
zero_before(c,Z) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Z = #count{C0 : C0 < c, input(0,C0,0)} .

%  extra = 1 if the column index c is > 0, otherwise 0
extra(c,Extra) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Extra = 1, c != 0 .
extra(c,Extra) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Extra = 0, c = 0 .

%  base = c  Z
base_val(c,Base) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    Base = c - Zc .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Two ways of placing a copy of the input
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  1)  K > 1   the copy is placed in two antidiagonal blocks:
%       topright block  (row block = 0 , col block = (K1)max_dim)
%       bottomleft block(row block = (K1)max_dim , col block = 0)

%  2)  K = 1   the copy is placed on the antidiagonal of the whole
%       output (a diagonal line of length max_dim  start).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Block generation (used when K > 1)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
Kminus1 = Kc - 1 .
block_col_offset = (Kminus1) * max_dim .           % col block offset
block_row_offset = (Kminus1) * max_dim .           % row block offset

%  For each nonzero cell we create two positions:
%  topright  :  (row = base + extra , col = (K1)max_dim + (max_dim1base))
%  bottomleft:  (row = (K1)max_dim + (max_dim1baseextra) , col = base + extra)

nonzero_top(R,C,V) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    row_top = base + Extra,
    col_top = max_dim - 1 - base,
    block_col = (Kminus1) * max_dim,
    R = row_top,
    C = block_col + col_top,
    V = V .
nonzero_bottom(R,C,V) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    row_bot = max_dim - 1 - base - Extra,
    col_bot = base + Extra,
    block_row = (Kminus1) * max_dim,
    R = block_row + row_bot,
    C = col_bot,
    V = V .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Diagonal generation (used when K = 1)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  startRow = base + extra + 1
diag_start(c,Start) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    Start = base + Extra + 1,
    maxR = max_dim - 1 .

%  i is the offset along the diagonal (0  maxRstart)
i_offset(I) :-
    I = 0 .
i_offset(I) :-
    I0 = I1,
    I = I1 + 1,
    I <= max_dim - 1 - Start .
diag_nonzero(R,C,V) :-
    input(0,c,V), V != 0,
    zero_before(c,Zc), base = c - Zc,
    extra(c,Extra),
    diag_start(c,Start),
    i_offset(I),
    R = Start + I,
    C = max_dim - 1 - I,
    V = V .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  has_nonzero  true if a nonzero colour must be placed here
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :-
    nonzero_top(R,_,_).
nonzero_bottom(R,_,_) :- true .   % every nonzero position will make has_nonzero true

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    diag_nonzero(R,C,Col), use_diag .
output(R,C,Col) :-
    nonzero_top(R,C,Col) ;   nonzero_bottom(R,C,Col) , not use_diag .
output(R,C,0) :-
    not has_nonzero(R,C) .      % all remaining cells stay 0

output(R,C) :-
    has_nonzero(R,C) .
output(R,C) :-
    not has_nonzero(R,C) .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cardinality  exactly one colour for each output cell
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output(R,C) .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary facts for the use_diag guard
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
use_diag :- Kc = 1 .
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour values (automatically supplied by the solver)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
colour(0..9) .
